stem: A company runs its own data center and is responsible for purchasing hardware, hiring facilities staff, and managing power and cooling. After migrating to AWS, which cost categories are eliminated?

options.label: A
options.text: Hardware procurement, data center facilities, and undifferentiated infrastructure operations

options.label: B
options.text: Network bandwidth costs between AWS services

options.label: C
options.text: Software licensing and application development costs

options.label: D
options.text: All costs, since AWS provides free compute for the first year

options.label: E
options.text: Only cooling and power costs — hardware is still purchased

explanation: On AWS, customers stop spending on physical hardware purchases, data center leases, power, cooling, and the staff required to operate physical infrastructure. These undifferentiated tasks are handled by AWS so customers can focus on their applications.

stem: An architect is reviewing a production system that stopped working when a single database server failed. Which cloud design principle would have prevented this outage?

options.label: A
options.text: Enable encryption — protect the database with KMS

options.label: B
options.text: Design for failure — assume components will fail and build redundancy

options.label: C
options.text: Implement elasticity — add more capacity when load increases

options.label: D
options.text: Use managed services — migrate to a fully managed database

options.label: E
options.text: Apply cost optimization — use Spot Instances for the database

explanation: The 'design for failure' principle assumes that any component can fail at any time. By building redundant components (multi-AZ database, standby replicas), the system continues running even when a single component fails.

stem: A retail company's on-premises infrastructure handles average daily traffic well but is overwhelmed during the holiday shopping season. Their on-premises team must provision for peak capacity all year long. Which cloud characteristic solves this most directly?

options.label: A
options.text: Elasticity — automatically scale resources up during peak and down after

options.label: B
options.text: High availability — multiple servers are always running

options.label: C
options.text: Durability — data is protected against loss during peak traffic

options.label: D
options.text: Security — traffic spikes don't compromise data integrity

options.label: E
options.text: Reliability — systems recover quickly when they fail

explanation: Elasticity lets you provision capacity for peak demand (holiday shopping) and release it when demand drops, so you pay only for what you actually use — rather than maintaining peak capacity year-round.

stem: A logistics company wants to launch a new application in a new country within weeks. Traditionally, expanding internationally requires purchasing hardware and leasing data center space, which takes months. Which cloud advantage enables rapid international expansion?

options.label: A
options.text: Economies of scale — AWS pricing decreases globally over time

options.label: B
options.text: Trade fixed expense for variable expense

options.label: C
options.text: Go global in minutes — deploy to AWS Regions worldwide

options.label: D
options.text: Stop spending money on data center operations

options.label: E
options.text: Stop guessing capacity — use only what you need

explanation: AWS has Regions in every major geography. Deploying to a new Region takes minutes via CloudFormation or the console — replacing the months needed to procure hardware and build a local data center presence.

stem: A startup chooses AWS over building its own data center. By using AWS, what shift occurs in how they think about infrastructure investment?

options.label: A
options.text: Infrastructure becomes a long-term capital investment, similar to buying property

options.label: B
options.text: Infrastructure is treated as a variable operating expense that scales with the business

options.label: C
options.text: Infrastructure costs are fixed and predictable regardless of usage

options.label: D
options.text: Infrastructure is free because AWS provides startup credits indefinitely

options.label: E
options.text: Infrastructure decisions are fully delegated to AWS with no customer input

explanation: Moving to AWS converts infrastructure from capital expenditure (purchasing assets that depreciate) to variable operational expenditure (paying only for what you consume). This aligns infrastructure costs with business growth and eliminates idle capacity spending.

stem: An application stores customer data that must legally remain within Germany. The architecture team selects the eu-central-1 (Frankfurt) Region. What guarantee does this Region selection provide?

options.label: A
options.text: All third-party services used will also stay in Germany, because AWS automatically enforces data residency for every dependency.

options.label: B
options.text: AWS will audit the application on the customer's behalf and certify GDPR compliance as part of the standard enterprise support agreement.

options.label: C
options.text: AWS guarantees sub-millisecond latency for German users by placing compute resources in the eu-central-1 Region near Frankfurt.

options.label: D
options.text: Data stored in eu-central-1 will not leave that Region unless the customer explicitly.

options.label: E
options.text: The application is automatically compliant with GDPR by virtue of running in an AWS Region within the European Union.

distractors.A: All third-party services used will also stay in Germany, because AWS automatically enforces data residency for every dependency.
distractors.B: AWS will audit the application on the customer's behalf and certify GDPR compliance as part of the standard enterprise support agreement.
distractors.C: AWS guarantees sub-millisecond latency for German users by placing compute resources in the eu-central-1 Region near Frankfurt.
distractors.E: The application is automatically compliant with GDPR by virtue of running in an AWS Region within the European Union.

explanation: AWS does not move data out of a Region you deploy to without your explicit configuration. Selecting eu-central-1 ensures data stays in Germany at the AWS infrastructure level — required for data residency compliance.

stem: What is the purpose of AWS Edge Locations, and how do they differ from AWS Regions?

options.label: A
options.text: Edge Locations are smaller points of presence used to cache content closer to users (CloudFront)

options.label: B
options.text: Edge Locations host EC2 instances for low-latency compute; Regions host only storage services

options.label: C
options.text: Edge Locations and Regions serve identical purposes but in different geographic areas

options.label: D
options.text: Edge Locations are used only for on-premises connectivity; Regions are for cloud workloads

options.label: E
options.text: Edge Locations are where AWS data centers are located; Regions are for content delivery

explanation: AWS has 30+ Regions for deploying full infrastructure stacks (EC2, RDS, etc.) and 400+ Edge Locations used by CloudFront and Route 53 to cache content and resolve DNS close to end users globally.

stem: A hospital's application must continue running even during a major infrastructure event that takes down an entire city block. How should this application be architected on AWS?

options.label: A
options.text: Enable CloudTrail in all Regions to ensure visibility during an outage

options.label: B
options.text: Deploy to a single large EC2 instance in one data center with redundant EBS volumes

options.label: C
options.text: Deploy to a single AZ with multiple EC2 instances for redundancy

options.label: D
options.text: Use Amazon S3 for all compute needs since it is automatically multi-AZ

options.label: E
options.text: Deploy across multiple Availability Zones within a Region — each AZ has independent power.

explanation: Each AWS Availability Zone has physically separate power, cooling, and networking from other AZs — typically miles apart. Distributing an application across multiple AZs means a localized disaster (fire, power failure) affecting one AZ does not impact the others.

stem: A company's security team is responsible for patching software on their Amazon EC2 instances. According to the Shared Responsibility Model, what aspect of the same EC2 deployment is AWS responsible for?

options.label: A
options.text: The application code running on the instances

options.label: B
options.text: The firewall rules configured in security groups

options.label: C
options.text: The physical host hardware, virtualization layer, and global network infrastructure

options.label: D
options.text: The IAM policies granting access to the instances

options.label: E
options.text: The data stored on EBS volumes attached to the instances

explanation: AWS manages all physical infrastructure: the servers, networking hardware, storage hardware, and the virtualization software (hypervisor) that EC2 runs on. The customer manages everything above the hypervisor — OS, applications, data, and network configurations.

stem: A SaaS company uses Amazon Lambda and DynamoDB. Under the Shared Responsibility Model, which security task is entirely AWS's responsibility for these services?

options.label: A
options.text: Writing secure code in Lambda functions

options.label: B
options.text: Securing the data stored in DynamoDB tables

options.label: C
options.text: Configuring VPC endpoint policies for DynamoDB access

options.label: D
options.text: Patching and securing the underlying infrastructure and runtimes that

options.label: E
options.text: Enabling DynamoDB encryption with customer-managed KMS keys

explanation: For managed and serverless services, AWS's responsibility extends further up the stack. AWS patches Lambda runtime environments, secures the DynamoDB service infrastructure, and manages the physical hardware — tasks the customer has no access or ability to perform.

stem: A company's engineering team spends 60% of their time on server maintenance, OS patching, and capacity planning. After migrating to managed AWS services, how does this change?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS provides a dedicated team to manage the infrastructure on the customer's behalf

options.label: B
options.text: The team can redirect their time to building features because AWS handles the undifferentiated infrastructure tasks

options.label: C
options.text: The team must monitor AWS service health to detect outages proactively

options.label: D
options.text: The workload is the same — managed services still require patching by the customer

options.label: E
options.text: The team must hire AWS-certified staff to maintain the managed services

explanation: AWS managed services handle undifferentiated heavy lifting (OS patching, hardware, scaling, availability). This frees engineering teams to focus on work that differentiates their business — building features, improving the product, and serving customers.

stem: A company deploying on AWS wants to implement infrastructure as code to ensure consistent environments across dev, staging, and production. Which approach aligns with this goal?

options.label: A
options.text: Use Trusted Advisor to copy configuration from one environment to another

options.label: B
options.text: Export production resource configurations as JSON and share them with the team

options.label: C
options.text: Copy production settings to staging manually after each change

options.label: D
options.text: Define all infrastructure in CloudFormation templates or CDK constructs, version-controlled in Git, and applied.

options.label: E
options.text: Manually configure each environment in the AWS Console and document the steps in a runbook

explanation: Infrastructure as code uses declarative templates (CloudFormation) or code (CDK) stored in Git. A CI/CD pipeline applies them consistently to all environments, eliminating manual steps and environment drift.

stem: Which AWS service enables customers to interact with multiple AWS services programmatically using consistent APIs, SDKs, and CLI tools — forming the foundation for automation and infrastructure as code?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Trusted Advisor provides real-time guidance to help you provision resources following AWS best practices across cost, security, and performance.

options.label: B
options.text: AWS CloudFormation lets you model and provision AWS and third-party resources in your cloud environment using infrastructure-as-code templates.

options.label: C
options.text: AWS CLI and AWS SDKs backed by the AWS API

options.label: D
options.text: The AWS Management Console is a web-based interface that provides a graphical view of your AWS resources and allows point-and-click management.

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon CloudWatch collects monitoring and operational data in the form of logs, metrics, and events from AWS resources and applications.

distractors.A: AWS Trusted Advisor provides real-time guidance to help you provision resources following AWS best practices across cost, security, and performance.
distractors.B: AWS CloudFormation lets you model and provision AWS and third-party resources in your cloud environment using infrastructure-as-code templates.
distractors.D: The AWS Management Console is a web-based interface that provides a graphical view of your AWS resources and allows point-and-click management.
distractors.E: Amazon CloudWatch collects monitoring and operational data in the form of logs, metrics, and events from AWS resources and applications.

explanation: The AWS API is the foundation for all interaction with AWS services. The CLI and SDKs (Python/Boto3, Java, Node.js, etc.) provide language-native access to these APIs, enabling automation, scripting, and IaC tooling to build on top.

stem: A well-architected application on AWS is designed so that component A does not directly call component B. Instead, component A writes to a queue, and B reads from it. Which design principle does this implement?

options.label: A
options.text: High availability

options.label: B
options.text: Defense in depth

options.label: C
options.text: Loose coupling

options.label: D
options.text: Immutable infrastructure

options.label: E
options.text: Serverless-first

explanation: Loose coupling decouples components by introducing an intermediary (queue, event bus, API) so they don't directly depend on each other's availability. If component B is down, A keeps writing to the queue — messages are not lost.

stem: A company runs a workload on AWS and has signed up for a Reserved Instance but their usage varies significantly. Some months the RI is fully utilized; other months their actual usage is far less than the reserved capacity. What happens to unused RI capacity?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS provides a credit for unused RI capacity that can be applied against future Reserved Instance purchases within the same account.

options.label: B
options.text: AWS automatically applies unused RI capacity to other eligible instances in the same account that match the RI's instance type, Region, and tenancy.

options.label: C
options.text: AWS refunds the unused portion of Reserved Instances at the end of each month on a pro-rated basis if usage falls below the reserved commitment level.

options.label: D
options.text: Unused RI capacity from one month rolls over and is automatically applied to usage in the following month until the term expires.

options.label: E
options.text: Unused RI capacity is not refunded — you pay for the full 1- or 3-year commitment regardless of whether you actually use the reserved capacity.

distractors.A: AWS provides a credit for unused RI capacity that can be applied against future Reserved Instance purchases within the same account.
distractors.B: AWS automatically applies unused RI capacity to other eligible instances in the same account that match the RI's instance type, Region, and tenancy.
distractors.C: AWS refunds the unused portion of Reserved Instances at the end of each month on a pro-rated basis if usage falls below the reserved commitment level.
distractors.D: Unused RI capacity from one month rolls over and is automatically applied to usage in the following month until the term expires.

explanation: A Reserved Instance is a billing commitment — you pay for the reserved capacity whether or not you use it. This is why RIs are best suited to workloads with predictable, steady utilization. Variable workloads may benefit more from Savings Plans.

stem: Which deployment model is being used when a company runs its customer-facing web application on AWS but keeps its core banking system on on-premises mainframes?

options.label: A
options.text: Community cloud deployment

options.label: B
options.text: Multi-cloud deployment

options.label: C
options.text: Hybrid cloud deployment

options.label: D
options.text: All-in cloud deployment

options.label: E
options.text: Private cloud deployment

explanation: Hybrid cloud combines on-premises (or private cloud) infrastructure with public cloud services. The web tier running on AWS while the mainframe stays on-premises is a classic hybrid architecture.

stem: A company's architecture review board asks why the dev team is provisioning 3x more EC2 instances than needed 'just in case' traffic grows. Which AWS advantage specifically makes this practice unnecessary?

options.label: A
options.text: Stop guessing capacity — provision the right amount and scale dynamically when demand changes

options.label: B
options.text: Global reach — traffic can be shifted to other regions if needed

options.label: C
options.text: High availability — extra instances automatically act as standby capacity

options.label: D
options.text: Economies of scale — more instances means more discounts

options.label: E
options.text: Trade fixed for variable expense — the extra instances are now variable cost

explanation: AWS Auto Scaling eliminates the need to over-provision by automatically adding capacity when demand grows and removing it when demand drops. There is no need to guess and hold idle instances 'just in case.'

stem: A systems architect describes a workload where each component can be replaced without the others knowing, as long as the interface contract is maintained. What AWS Well-Architected concept does this describe?

options.label: A
options.text: Loose coupling through well-defined interfaces ensures components interact only via stable APIs, so changes to one component do not break others.

options.label: B
options.text: Defense in depth applies multiple overlapping security layers so that if one control fails, additional controls continue to protect the system.

options.label: C
options.text: Fault tolerance keeps a system operating correctly even when individual components fail, ensuring continuous availability without manual intervention.

options.label: D
options.text: Scalability is the ability to handle increasing load by adding resources, either vertically through larger instances or horizontally through more instances.

options.label: E
options.text: Using services instead of servers means leveraging AWS managed offerings so you focus on application logic rather than maintaining underlying infrastructure.

distractors.B: Defense in depth applies multiple overlapping security layers so that if one control fails, additional controls continue to protect the system.
distractors.C: Fault tolerance keeps a system operating correctly even when individual components fail, ensuring continuous availability without manual intervention.
distractors.D: Scalability is the ability to handle increasing load by adding resources, either vertically through larger instances or horizontally through more instances.
distractors.E: Using services instead of servers means leveraging AWS managed offerings so you focus on application logic rather than maintaining underlying infrastructure.

explanation: Loose coupling through well-defined interfaces (APIs, events, queues) means components depend only on each other's interface — not their internal implementation. Components can be replaced, upgraded, or scaled independently as long as the interface contract is maintained.

stem: A company wants to evaluate if their AWS architecture meets best practices across operational excellence, security, reliability, performance efficiency, and cost optimization. Which AWS tool guides this evaluation?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Well-Architected Tool

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon Inspector

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Config

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Trusted Advisor

options.label: E
options.text: AWS Security Hub

explanation: The AWS Well-Architected Tool guides customers through structured workload reviews against the six pillars of the Well-Architected Framework: Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, and Sustainability.

stem: A company needs high availability for its application hosted on EC2. Which statement best describes what 'high availability' means in an AWS context?

options.label: A
options.text: The application is designed to minimize downtime by operating through component failures

options.label: B
options.text: The application data is backed up to S3 every hour

options.label: C
options.text: AWS guarantees 100% uptime for all EC2 instances

options.label: D
options.text: The application is replicated to every AWS Region simultaneously

options.label: E
options.text: The application never experiences any latency under any conditions

explanation: High availability means the system remains operational through failures. Multi-AZ deployments with load balancers achieve this — if one AZ fails, traffic automatically reroutes to healthy instances in other AZs with minimal downtime.

stem: A company is conducting an AWS training session on the Shared Responsibility Model. Which TWO statements correctly describe the division of responsibilities for Amazon S3? (Select TWO)

options.label: A
options.text: AWS is responsible for the physical security of the data centers where S3 data is stored

options.label: B
options.text: AWS is responsible for the content of objects stored in the customer's S3 buckets

options.label: C
options.text: The customer is responsible for the durability of S3 — ensuring data is replicated

options.label: D
options.text: The customer is responsible for configuring S3 bucket policies and access controls

options.label: E
options.text: AWS is responsible for the network equipment connecting S3 to the internet

explanation: For S3: AWS manages the physical infrastructure, replication, and service durability. The customer manages bucket policies, ACLs, encryption settings, lifecycle rules, and the data itself — all the 'in the cloud' controls.

stem: A company is evaluating the six pillars of the AWS Well-Architected Framework. Which TWO pillars focus specifically on keeping costs low and systems running reliably? (Select TWO)

options.label: A
options.text: Cost Optimization

options.label: B
options.text: Performance Efficiency

options.label: C
options.text: Reliability

options.label: D
options.text: Security

options.label: E
options.text: Operational Excellence

explanation: Cost Optimization focuses on avoiding unnecessary costs and maximizing value from spend. Reliability focuses on ensuring workloads perform their intended functions correctly and consistently, including recovery from failures.
